researchTasks: Read the parent issue, blockers, linked ADRs/product documents, current implementation, and recent relevant pull requests before choosing an approach.
Prefer official documentation, primary sources, repository evidence, and measured behavior; record UNKNOWN — REQUIRES VALIDATION instead of inventing missing facts.
setupSteps: Work from a clean, isolated branch; install the pinned repository prerequisites and confirm the documented baseline checks before changing behavior.
List the exact files, routes, data records, commands, external services, and human approvals affected by this issue before implementation starts.
implementationSteps: Deliver the smallest end-to-end slice that satisfies the issue outcome; avoid unrelated refactors and speculative infrastructure.
Keep boundaries explicit so UI, catalog/data tooling, preserved Rust/WASM code, and release automation can be tested independently.
uiUxTasks: State the user-facing impact explicitly; when UI is in scope, cover loading, empty, error, offline, keyboard, responsive, zoom, reduced-motion, and missing-media states.
Use the approved original Arcaea-inspired design system and do not copy Sekai Viewer, official game UI, or third-party wiki layouts or assets.
dataBackendTasks: The Web MVP defaults to schema-validated, versioned static data and build-time tooling; do not introduce a hosted backend, account system, or database without an approved post-MVP trigger.
Preserve provenance, stable IDs, reference integrity, deterministic generation, and rollback to the last reviewed catalog or build.
soloExecution: Execute in this order: research and evidence capture → decision or design note → failing test/check → minimal implementation → targeted verification → full applicable checks → PR evidence and human review.
Keep each work session reviewable for one maintainer; split newly discovered independent work into linked follow-up issues instead of expanding this issue silently.
validationSteps: Run the narrowest relevant test first, then all checks required by the changed files; code or automation changes require `pnpm check`, and roadmap changes also require the roadmap shell tests and live dry-run.
Perform the manual acceptance flow from a clean browser or checkout and record exact commands, observed results, screenshots or reports, and any approved exception.
deliverables: A focused pull request with intentional commits, updated documentation/data, and an acceptance-criteria checklist linked to reviewable evidence.
A concise record of decisions, rejected alternatives, residual risks, ownership, and the next dependency unlocked by completion.
risksAndRollback: Do not hide legal, accessibility, security, performance, privacy, or maintenance uncertainty; record the exception owner and follow-up before merge.
Rollback by reverting the focused change and restoring the last validated catalog, asset manifest, configuration, or deployment artifact; document any non-automatic recovery step.

researchTasks: Compare the six-week issue graph with the accepted product brief, current repository state, maintainer capacity, and the Sekai Viewer findings that are genuinely reusable.
implementationSteps: Keep one critical path from discovery through public preview, ensure every phase gate has evidence, and move runtime/backend expansion behind explicit post-MVP decisions.
uiUxTasks: Verify the roadmap includes original UI research, design tokens, representative prototypes, accessibility, responsive behavior, and measured performance rather than a visual clone.
dataBackendTasks: Verify the critical path is static-catalog-first and that hosted services are represented only by measured decision triggers.
validationSteps: Audit all managed issues for owner, milestone, parent, blockers, acceptance criteria, execution guidance, and explicit MVP/non-MVP boundaries.
deliverables: Current issue hierarchy export, dependency audit, capacity/scope-cut table, and links to the canonical brief, design decisions, and release gate.
risksAndRollback: If six weeks becomes unrealistic, cut P1/P2 enhancements first while retaining legal provenance, catalog correctness, core routes, accessibility, security, and rollback readiness.

researchTasks: Confirm product, UI, data, legal, and workflow decisions are resolved before foundation code begins.
implementationSteps: Sequence charter/PRD/legal/UI/data decisions, surface contradictions, and close the gate only after human-required approvals are recorded.
uiUxTasks: Require research evidence and prototypes for home, explore, and song detail before approving reusable UI foundations.
dataBackendTasks: Require a static catalog ADR, provenance policy, and legal seed-data contract before implementation.
validationSteps: Run a gate review that samples every Week 1 deliverable and confirms Week 2 issues are unblocked without hidden decisions.
deliverables: Week 1 gate report with approved decisions, rejected alternatives, unresolved risks, and explicit go/no-go status.

researchTasks: Review sustainable solo-maintainer workflows, existing repository conventions, GPL obligations, fan-project wording, and comparable community contribution policies.
implementationSteps: Write the charter, contribution workflow, Definition of Ready, Definition of Done, human/AI responsibility boundaries, correction path, and links from the README.
uiUxTasks: Ensure contributor-facing documents are scannable, keyboard-readable, jargon-controlled, and provide examples for data, documentation, accessibility, and code contributions.
dataBackendTasks: Define how facts, sources, assets, generated catalog outputs, and future service decisions remain reviewable in repository history.
validationSteps: Tabletop-test one factual correction and one small UI change from issue selection through merge evidence; resolve every ambiguity found.
deliverables: `PROJECT_CHARTER.md`, aligned `CONTRIBUTING.md`, reusable readiness/done checklists, README links, and maintainer approval evidence.
risksAndRollback: Reject process that requires enterprise staffing or private-chat context; simplify any workflow that cannot be followed in roughly three focused hours per day.

researchTasks: Interview or model ordinary-player lookup and discovery tasks, inspect current Arcaea information gaps, and compare at least several catalog/wiki products without adopting their information architecture blindly.
implementationSteps: Write personas, prioritized journeys, route taxonomy, page responsibilities, URL-state contract, non-goals, success measures, and acceptance scenarios.
uiUxTasks: Define desktop-first and narrow-screen journeys for home, explore, song, pack, partner, story, game topic, information, settings, legal, and error surfaces.
dataBackendTasks: Map every route to static catalog entities/indexes and identify which unknown or current information cannot be represented safely in MVP data.
validationSteps: Walk through primary journeys using wireframes and sample records; verify every filter/sort state is shareable and runtime/backend features are absent.
deliverables: Approved PRD, sitemap, user-flow diagrams, route/data matrix, explicit cuts, and measurable acceptance scenarios.
risksAndRollback: If a route lacks a validated user task or legal data path, remove it from MVP navigation and retain it as a post-MVP candidate.

researchTasks: Study at least eight references across game databases, documentation, data explorers, responsive galleries, and accessible information products; include Sekai Viewer as only one reference.
setupSteps: Capture comparable desktop/mobile screenshots or notes, establish a contrast-testing method, and prepare low-fidelity and high-fidelity design files with reusable tokens.
implementationSteps: Produce information-architecture findings, component/state inventory, two theme directions, wireframes, high-fidelity home/explore/song prototypes, and rejected alternatives.
uiUxTasks: Specify navigation density, search/filter behavior, card/table switching, image framing, spoiler/unknown states, focus order, 200% zoom, reduced motion, and touch targets.
dataBackendTasks: Prototype with representative normalized view models, long titles, missing images, uncertain constants, stale catalog status, and large result counts rather than idealized mock data.
validationSteps: Run preliminary WCAG contrast checks, keyboard walkthroughs, narrow-width review, performance/image assumptions review, and human approval against originality criteria.
deliverables: Research matrix, accepted visual direction, tokens, component inventory, state matrix, annotated prototypes, and reasons each rejected direction was declined.
risksAndRollback: Remove decorative effects that reduce readability or performance; fall back to neutral accessible primitives while preserving original Arcaea-inspired accents.

researchTasks: Measure expected MVP record counts, update frequency, query/filter requirements, GitHub Pages constraints, browser caching, static JSON patterns from Sekai Viewer, and migration triggers for hosted search/API.
implementationSteps: Write an ADR defining entity boundaries, stable IDs, manifest/version fields, generated indexes/view models, loading API, stale/corrupt behavior, atomic publication, and future migration seams.
uiUxTasks: Define user-visible loading, unsupported-version, stale snapshot, partial-media, offline, and recovery messages before coding the loader.
dataBackendTasks: Choose static versioned artifacts for MVP, keep raw reviewed records separate from generated UI indexes, and define backend triggers using measured latency, dataset size, update volume, or collaboration needs.
validationSteps: Prototype loading representative catalog files under the GitHub Pages base path and verify deterministic output, caching semantics, failure states, and no hidden network database dependency.
deliverables: Accepted ADR, data-access interfaces, version compatibility table, failure-state contract, and hosted-service decision triggers.
risksAndRollback: If generated indexes become too large, split by domain or route before considering a server; retain a reader for the previous schema version during migration.

researchTasks: Review official Arcaea/lowiro terms and public sources, repository GPL scope, source-license compatibility, fan-content/takedown practices, and permission requirements for images, audio, charts, and prose.
implementationSteps: Define source hierarchy, fact/prose/media rules, source and asset record schemas, forbidden content, correction/takedown flow, disclaimer, and human approval points.
uiUxTasks: Specify visible provenance/review indicators, uncertainty wording, spoiler treatment, alt-text ownership, legal page placement, and a usable correction path.
dataBackendTasks: Require every published fact group and media asset to resolve to reviewable source/permission records; never store credentials or proprietary source files as evidence.
validationSteps: Audit a sample song, partner, story, and image record end-to-end; have the maintainer approve ambiguous licensing and publication decisions explicitly.
deliverables: Legal/provenance policy, source hierarchy, asset permission convention, forbidden-content list, disclaimer, correction/takedown process, and sample reviewed records.
risksAndRollback: When permission or attribution is uncertain, publish a neutral placeholder or omit the asset/content until evidence is approved; support prompt removal without a code redesign.

researchTasks: Inventory required fields and relationships for songs, charts, packs, partners, story, game topics, releases, sources, and assets; distinguish verified facts, maintainer summaries, unknowns, and version applicability.
setupSteps: Choose the schema/validation library already compatible with the planned TypeScript toolchain and prepare valid, boundary, and invalid fixtures without protected charts or audio.
implementationSteps: Define schemas, stable ID rules, cross-reference validators, size limits, review/provenance fields, seed records, generated type outputs, and deterministic validation commands.
uiUxTasks: Ensure schemas represent long/multilingual labels, unknown values, missing media, spoiler flags, aliases, and human-readable validation errors needed by every page template.
dataBackendTasks: Model normalized source records and build-time view-model generation rather than performing repeated multi-file joins in the browser.
validationSteps: Test missing IDs, duplicates, broken references, invalid URLs/protocols, unreviewed assets, unsupported versions, oversized content, and deterministic seed output.
deliverables: Versioned schemas, type generation, validator, legal seed catalog covering every page template, invalid fixture corpus, and schema documentation.
risksAndRollback: Avoid encoding speculative future fields; version incompatible changes and retain the previous validated seed snapshot for rollback.

researchTasks: Confirm all Week 1 decisions are approved and map each foundation issue to the smallest deployable wiki shell/catalog slice.
implementationSteps: Order quality gate, build migration, shell, design system, catalog loader, and asset pipeline so each produces independently testable output.
uiUxTasks: Require representative loading/error/empty/offline states and both themes in the foundation showcase before feature pages depend on them.
dataBackendTasks: Keep static data and media pipelines independent from UI components and prohibit speculative hosted services.
validationSteps: Run the Week 2 gate from a clean checkout and prove a validated seed record renders through the public shell under the deployment base path.
deliverables: Week 2 gate report, foundation architecture map, baseline bundle/build measurements, and next-phase readiness evidence.

researchTasks: Inventory every Rust, WASM, frontend, content, roadmap, and build command; identify duplicated checks, platform differences, warning policy, and generated artifacts.
setupSteps: Verify pinned Rust, wasm target, Node, pnpm, jq, Bash/WSL, browser-test, and image-tool prerequisites from a clean Windows/WSL-compatible checkout.
implementationSteps: Create one documented quality command and aligned least-privilege CI jobs for format, lint, types, tests, catalog validation, Rust/WASM preservation, production build, and smoke checks.
uiUxTasks: Make failure output actionable for a solo maintainer and ensure public builds cannot expose debug/runtime routes or fixtures.
dataBackendTasks: Run schema/reference/asset validation before bundling and verify generated outputs are deterministic and intentionally tracked or ignored.
validationSteps: Run the command twice from clean state, inject representative failures for each stage, and compare CI/local behavior on supported environments.
deliverables: One-command quality gate, CI workflow, prerequisite/setup guide, failure-troubleshooting table, and clean-checkout evidence.
risksAndRollback: Split slow checks into clear CI jobs without weakening the merge gate; revert newly added tooling if it duplicates existing reliable checks without measurable value.

researchTasks: Review approved routes/prototypes, GitHub Pages routing options, theme initialization, error-boundary patterns, focus management, and minimal routing dependencies.
setupSteps: Create representative route fixtures, configure base-path testing, and define app-version/catalog-version diagnostics before replacing the debug screen.
implementationSteps: Build semantic header/navigation/footer, skip link, route layout, lazy route boundaries, settings/theme persistence, 404, loading, error, offline, and recovery states.
uiUxTasks: Test desktop navigation density, narrow-screen menu, visible focus, logical landmarks/headings, no-theme flash, direct-link recovery, and settings accessibility.
dataBackendTasks: Expose only a catalog repository interface to route components; keep Rust/WASM imports and hidden viewer fixtures outside the public entry bundle.
validationSteps: Unit-test settings and route states, keyboard-test all navigation, build with the repository base path, and smoke-test direct URLs/refresh on the selected Pages strategy.
deliverables: Public app shell, route map, settings/theme module, resilient state components, route tests, and bundle evidence excluding runtime surfaces.
risksAndRollback: If Pages history routing is unreliable, use the approved hash or fallback strategy rather than adding a server; retain the previous debug entry separately for local runtime work.

researchTasks: Compare current Vite-specific behavior with current Rsbuild/Rspack documentation, React 19 support, CSS/Tailwind integration, test tooling, asset imports, base paths, WASM isolation, and bundle analysis.
setupSteps: Capture current dev-start/build time, bundle composition, environment variables, aliases, raw fixture imports, and production output before changing configuration.
implementationSteps: Write the ADR, migrate scripts/config/plugins incrementally, preserve test commands and base-path output, remove Vite-only paths, and document rollback.
uiUxTasks: Verify HMR, error overlays, route loading, CSS ordering, theme initialization, and public asset URLs behave equivalently.
dataBackendTasks: Ensure static catalog and generated asset paths remain deterministic and runtime WASM is not pulled into the public bundle.
validationSteps: Compare before/after development startup, production build, bundle report, source maps, tests, GitHub Pages base path, and clean-install behavior.
deliverables: Migration ADR, Rsbuild configuration, updated scripts/lockfile/docs, benchmark comparison, bundle report, and removal list for obsolete Vite code.
risksAndRollback: Keep migration commits isolated so the prior Vite configuration can be restored if required plugins, tests, or deployment paths regress.

researchTasks: Translate the approved prototypes into semantic tokens and minimal primitives; verify Tailwind CSS v4 integration, font licensing, contrast, zoom, motion, and high-density data presentation.
setupSteps: Create a component showcase with realistic long text, unknown data, missing images, errors, filters, tables, cards, focus states, and both themes.
implementationSteps: Implement semantic color/type/spacing/elevation/border/motion tokens and focused primitives for navigation, buttons, links, forms, cards, tables, filters, badges, media frames, prose, and feedback.
uiUxTasks: Document API, state variants, focus behavior, responsive rules, reduced-motion behavior, and misuse examples; prefer composition over a heavy component framework.
dataBackendTasks: Keep presentation components independent of raw catalog schemas by consuming typed view models and explicit unknown/provenance states.
validationSteps: Run automated contrast/accessibility checks, keyboard and 200% zoom review, narrow-width snapshots, reduced-motion review, and bundle-cost comparison.
deliverables: Token source, primitive library, showcase, usage documentation, accessibility results, and visual regression references for both themes.
risksAndRollback: Remove custom polygon/motion effects that harm readability or bundle cost; fall back to semantic CSS primitives without blocking feature work.

researchTasks: Use the approved data ADR and schemas to define browser loading, cache keys, compatibility, generated index size, failure recovery, and SSR-free GitHub Pages constraints.
setupSteps: Prepare valid, stale, corrupt, missing, unsupported-version, and cross-reference-invalid catalog fixtures plus a local static-server test path.
implementationSteps: Implement build-time validation/generation, manifest checksums, typed catalog repository, domain indexes/view models, cache/version handling, and explicit error classes/states.
uiUxTasks: Provide actionable loading, retry, stale, unsupported-version, offline, and partial-media messages without exposing internal stack traces.
dataBackendTasks: Keep raw normalized records, generated route indexes, and media metadata separate; perform joins at build time when practical and avoid runtime network databases.
validationSteps: Test deterministic generation, integrity failures, cache/version transitions, base-path loading, offline last-good snapshot behavior, and representative query performance.
deliverables: Catalog validator/generator, typed repository API, versioned artifacts, fixtures, failure-state components/contracts, and performance measurements.
risksAndRollback: If a generated index exceeds budget, split by domain or lazy route artifact; keep the last-good manifest and reader available for immediate rollback.

researchTasks: Inventory permitted asset sources and terms, required page breakpoints, modern image-format support, GitHub Pages caching, checksums, duplicate detection, metadata stripping, and alt-text ownership.
setupSteps: Prepare licensed/open sample originals, placeholder assets, image tooling, deterministic output directories, and size/dimension budgets without importing protected game bundles.
implementationSteps: Define asset records and approval status, validate permissions/checksums/dimensions/alt decisions, generate responsive AVIF/WebP variants, and publish deterministic paths/manifests.
uiUxTasks: Reserve aspect ratio and dimensions, define art-direction/cropping, loading/failure placeholders, decorative-vs-informative alt rules, and reduced-data behavior.
dataBackendTasks: Use asset IDs/resolvers instead of hard-coded remote URLs; keep source originals according to permission terms and publish only reviewed derivatives.
validationSteps: Reject missing approval, checksum drift, duplicates, oversized output, invalid dimensions, unsafe paths, and missing alt decisions; measure layout shift and transfer size on representative pages.
deliverables: Asset schema/manifest, permission-evidence convention, optimization commands, reviewed samples, placeholder strategy, budgets, and audit report.
risksAndRollback: Omit or replace uncertain media immediately; restore the previous asset manifest and generated directory if optimization or licensing review fails.

researchTasks: Confirm foundation readiness and prioritize the home-to-explore-to-detail journey over secondary content.
implementationSteps: Coordinate shared templates, query-state utilities, catalog view models, provenance UI, and route tests before page-specific polish.
uiUxTasks: Require coherent list/detail navigation and complete states across all Week 3 pages.
dataBackendTasks: Use generated catalog indexes and keep page code free of repeated raw multi-file joins.
validationSteps: Run the full primary journey against seed data, missing/uncertain records, keyboard navigation, and the production base path.
deliverables: Week 3 gate report, journey evidence, page-state matrix, and performance snapshot.

researchTasks: Map shared player questions and related-entity relationships across songs, packs, partners, story, and game topics.
implementationSteps: Define reusable page shells, provenance blocks, related-link patterns, media states, and domain view-model contracts before implementing every route separately.
uiUxTasks: Prevent dead ends, preserve back/share context, and keep dense facts readable without copying conventional wiki clutter.
dataBackendTasks: Require all public page data to come from validated static records/indexes and expose unknown/version state explicitly.
validationSteps: Test home → explore → detail → related entity loops and representative missing-data paths.
deliverables: Shared page-template contracts, relation map, state matrix, and cross-route acceptance evidence.

researchTasks: Identify the minimum current/highlight information ordinary players need on landing, and verify each candidate module has a legal, versioned data source.
setupSteps: Prepare seed release/event/featured records, licensed or placeholder hero media, and narrow/slow/offline test scenarios.
implementationSteps: Build latest release/event, search entry, featured catalog links, concise project/game introduction, update status, disclaimer, and contribution path using reusable modules.
uiUxTasks: Keep primary discovery actions visible early, maintain text readability over media, avoid carousel dependency, and test keyboard/screen-reader order and responsive art treatment.
dataBackendTasks: Select featured/current content from reviewed catalog configuration rather than hard-coded component constants or a live backend.
validationSteps: Test current, no-current-event, missing-image, stale-catalog, offline, keyboard, narrow viewport, and production image/bundle budgets.
deliverables: Homepage route, reusable modules, catalog selection rules, metadata, route tests, accessibility evidence, and performance screenshot/report.
risksAndRollback: If current information cannot be maintained reliably, show the last reviewed release and catalog timestamp instead of an inaccurate live-looking module.

researchTasks: Analyze player search/filter tasks, expected dataset size, URLSearchParams behavior, locale/case normalization, deterministic sorting, and whether virtualization is measurably necessary.
setupSteps: Prepare varied records with aliases, long titles, equal sort keys, unknown constants, unavailable charts, and no-result combinations.
implementationSteps: Implement query parser/serializer, generated searchable view models, title/artist search, pack/difficulty/level/constant/BPM/availability filters, deterministic sort, reset, count, and no-result guidance.
uiUxTasks: Make controls keyboard-friendly, label active filters, preserve shareable state, support compact card/table presentation as approved, and avoid filter panels that dominate narrow screens.
dataBackendTasks: Perform indexing at build time, keep filter logic pure/testable, and add a hosted search service only after measured static limits exceed the approved budget.
validationSteps: Property/fixture-test query round trips and stable sort, manually test deep links/refresh/back-forward, and benchmark representative full-catalog filtering.
deliverables: Explore route, typed URL-state module, pure search/filter/sort functions, generated index contract, tests, and performance measurements.
risksAndRollback: If advanced filters or multiple view modes threaten the schedule, retain search plus the highest-value filters and record deferred controls separately.

researchTasks: List common song/pack lookup questions and verify source/unknown behavior for title, artist, release, BPM, charts, difficulties, constants, note counts, aliases, and media.
setupSteps: Prepare complete, minimal, uncertain, future/unavailable, missing-art, and broken-related-entity fixtures.
implementationSteps: Build typed song and pack view models, semantic detail layouts, chart facts table, provenance/review block, related navigation, pack song list, metadata hooks, and domain not-found states.
uiUxTasks: Keep dense chart facts scannable at zoom/narrow widths, distinguish unknown from absent, reserve jacket dimensions, and exclude AFF/playback/analytics controls.
dataBackendTasks: Resolve bidirectional relations and derived display values during generation; page routes consume one prepared view model rather than joining raw files repeatedly.
validationSteps: Test complete/missing/uncertain records, bidirectional links, invalid IDs, images, keyboard table navigation, responsive layout, SEO hooks, and production base path.
deliverables: Song and pack routes, view-model generators, chart facts component, provenance UI, fixtures/tests, and representative page evidence.
risksAndRollback: Hide unsupported fields instead of guessing; use placeholders or omit media when permission is not approved.

researchTasks: Prioritize encyclopedia topics by validated user need, legal content path, and seed-data readiness.
implementationSteps: Reuse Week 3 page/data primitives for partners, story, game topics, and the version-update workflow; reject one-off infrastructure.
uiUxTasks: Require readable long-form/spoiler patterns and consistent related navigation.
dataBackendTasks: Prove one repeatable versioned content update and rollback without scraping or a production database.
validationSteps: Audit representative pages and execute a sample release update end-to-end.
deliverables: Week 4 gate report, coverage matrix, sample update diff, and unresolved content/legal gaps.

researchTasks: Identify verified partner/character fields, aliases, versioned attributes, related content, spoiler boundaries, and permitted art sources.
setupSteps: Prepare complete, art-less, spoiler-sensitive, alias-heavy, and version-specific partner fixtures.
implementationSteps: Build index/detail view models, concise verified facts, maintainer-summary separation, aliases, version/review indicators, related story/packs/releases, and licensed/placeholder media.
uiUxTasks: Provide accessible spoiler disclosure, image fallback, clear fact-vs-summary styling, keyboard index navigation, and no dead-end related links.
dataBackendTasks: Generate partner indexes and reverse relations at build time; retain source/asset IDs and version applicability in each view model.
validationSteps: Test spoiler controls with keyboard/assistive technology, missing art, uncertain data, aliases/search, cross-links, and responsive detail layouts.
deliverables: Partner index/detail routes, view models, spoiler/provenance components, fixtures/tests, and content/asset review evidence.
risksAndRollback: Omit unsourced lore or art; publish a concise placeholder record until reviewed information becomes available.

researchTasks: Define story taxonomy/chronology from official sources, acceptable original-summary length, spoiler levels, uncertainty representation, and related entities.
setupSteps: Prepare short/long, locked/spoiler, uncertain-order, missing-relation, and table-of-contents fixtures using original placeholder prose.
implementationSteps: Build story index/detail view models, chronology filters, spoiler disclosure, concise original summaries, provenance/version blocks, related links, and accessible long-form layout.
uiUxTasks: Test readable measure/line-height, heading hierarchy, table of contents, focus after disclosure, copy/share behavior, and narrow/200% zoom layouts.
dataBackendTasks: Store summaries and chronology metadata as reviewed static records; never ingest or redistribute unlicensed long-form game/wiki text.
validationSteps: Editorial originality review, source/chronology audit, keyboard/screen-reader spoiler tests, long-page performance, and broken-relation checks.
deliverables: Story taxonomy, index/detail routes, original seed summaries, spoiler/TOC components, fixtures/tests, and editorial/provenance review.
risksAndRollback: If chronology or summary rights are uncertain, label uncertainty and reduce content to sourced factual metadata rather than reproducing text.

researchTasks: Inventory player questions and official/versioned sources for World Mode, Course Mode, achievements, and foundational mechanics; reject topics without maintainable evidence.
setupSteps: Prepare topic fixtures with aliases, version applicability, related entities, diagrams absent, and superseded mechanics.
implementationSteps: Define topic taxonomy/template, original concise explanations, applicability/review metadata, related navigation, information hub, and search index entries.
uiUxTasks: Use semantic sections, definition lists/tables only where appropriate, anchor navigation for long topics, and explicit outdated/unknown callouts.
dataBackendTasks: Generate topic/alias indexes and keep versioned facts in static records; avoid one component or schema per topic.
validationSteps: Review factual accuracy/version labels, template consistency, search aliases, broken links, keyboard anchors, and representative long/narrow layouts.
deliverables: Topic taxonomy/schema additions, reusable topic route/template, initial reviewed topics, search integration, tests, and source audit.
risksAndRollback: Cut low-confidence or maintenance-heavy topics first; retain a small high-quality encyclopedia rather than broad stale coverage.

researchTasks: Map official release/version sources, update cadence, current manual catalog process, change categories, reviewer capacity, and legal/media review requirements.
setupSteps: Create a sandbox version snapshot and sample official update containing added, changed, removed, uncertain, and media-change records.
implementationSteps: Define intake issue template, source capture, normalized edits, generated diff/summary, schema/reference/link/permission/editorial checks, snapshot publication, changelog, and rollback procedure.
uiUxTasks: Define how users see catalog version, freshness, changed/removed content, stale data, and corrections without presenting unreviewed updates as current.
dataBackendTasks: Automate validation and diffing but never fact invention; keep reviewed version snapshots immutable and switch current publication atomically.
validationSteps: Execute the sample update end-to-end, deliberately fail each check, verify diff readability, publish a test snapshot, and dry-run rollback to the prior version.
deliverables: Release-update template, update runbook, diff generator/summary, validation pipeline, sample snapshot/changelog, and rollback evidence.
risksAndRollback: If an update is partially reviewed, keep the prior current snapshot and publish uncertainty only when the policy explicitly permits it.

researchTasks: Collect measured accessibility, responsive, performance, security, offline, SEO, and operational baselines for representative routes.
implementationSteps: Prioritize P0 defects and production risks; keep PWA/telemetry/SEO enhancements from masking core content or legal failures.
uiUxTasks: Require real-device/browser and assistive-technology evidence for primary flows.
dataBackendTasks: Audit catalog/asset/build supply chain and last-good snapshot recovery.
validationSteps: Run a consolidated quality review and record target, observed value, owner, and disposition for every exception.
deliverables: Week 5 quality report, budgets, threat model, exception register, and release blockers list.

researchTasks: Establish WCAG-oriented criteria, supported browser/viewports, image and JS budgets, Core Web Vitals targets, assistive-technology checks, and current measured baselines.
setupSteps: Prepare automated axe/Lighthouse or equivalent checks, keyboard scripts, 200%/400% zoom cases, slow-network profiles, and representative heavy pages.
implementationSteps: Fix semantic/focus/name/contrast/zoom/motion issues, responsive overflow, image dimensions/formats/loading, route chunking, and measured long-task/layout-shift causes.
uiUxTasks: Test all primary flows keyboard-only and screen-reader landmarks/names; preserve content order and functionality at narrow widths and high zoom.
dataBackendTasks: Reduce generated payload/index/media size before adding runtime complexity; record catalog and asset contributions to each page budget.
validationSteps: Run automated and manual audits on home/explore/song/pack/partner/story/topic/settings/legal/error/offline surfaces and publish before/after measurements.
deliverables: Accessibility audit, performance report, fixed defects, budgets enforced in CI where stable, screenshots/traces, and approved exception register.
risksAndRollback: Disable costly visual effects, nonessential prefetching, or P1 features that prevent P0 accessibility/performance targets.

researchTasks: Evaluate actual offline user value, service-worker lifecycle, GitHub Pages caching, catalog/asset versioning, storage quotas, update prompts, takedown invalidation, and browser support.
setupSteps: Prepare two catalog versions, removed asset/content cases, offline/low-storage scenarios, and a clean way to inspect/unregister caches.
implementationSteps: Cache only the app shell and approved last-good versioned artifacts, implement update/stale/storage-error UI, and define activation/cleanup rules that cannot retain removed assets indefinitely.
uiUxTasks: Make install/update/offline states optional and understandable, preserve online recovery, and avoid blocking content with aggressive prompts.
dataBackendTasks: Key caches by immutable catalog/asset versions and keep legal removal/version revocation outside indefinitely cached opaque URLs.
validationSteps: Test first visit, repeat visit, update, failed update, offline navigation, quota failure, removed asset, cache cleanup, and service-worker rollback across supported browsers.
deliverables: PWA decision/implementation, cache policy, update/offline UI, tests, storage/takedown notes, and rollback/unregister runbook.
risksAndRollback: If lifecycle or legal invalidation cannot be made reliable before release, ship without PWA and retain normal browser caching only.

researchTasks: Threat-model static catalog content, Markdown/prose rendering, external URLs, images/SVG, service workers, dependencies, Actions, generated artifacts, secrets, and GitHub Pages header limitations.
setupSteps: Inventory dependencies/actions/permissions, configure secret/dependency/content scans, and prepare unsafe protocol/HTML/path/SVG/manifest fixtures.
implementationSteps: Enforce safe URL/content schemas, avoid arbitrary HTML, pin/minimize actions/dependencies, add least-privilege workflows, dependency review, secret checks, SECURITY.md, and compensating controls.
uiUxTasks: Provide safe user-facing error/recovery states without leaking stack traces or encouraging unsafe copy/paste; clearly distinguish external links.
dataBackendTasks: Treat catalog and generated assets as untrusted build inputs, validate protocols/paths/content, and never place secrets or personal data in static outputs.
validationSteps: Execute the threat-model test cases, inspect workflow token permissions, dependency diff, generated bundle/content, service-worker scope, and hosting headers/limitations.
deliverables: Threat model, hardened validators/workflows, dependency/action inventory, security policy, test fixtures, residual-risk report, and incident/rollback notes.
risksAndRollback: Remove or render-as-text any content feature that cannot be sanitized reliably; disable vulnerable workflow/service-worker paths until corrected.

researchTasks: Determine the minimum diagnostics needed for a static site, privacy expectations, issue-report friction, uptime/link/freshness checks, and whether telemetry provides enough value to justify consent/operations.
implementationSteps: Define error boundary/support bundle fields, catalog/app versions, redaction guidance, issue forms, no-telemetry or opt-in decision, and lightweight documented health checks.
uiUxTasks: Create actionable error pages with retry/home/report paths and a copyable support summary that excludes personal browsing data, local files, secrets, and full URLs where unsafe.
dataBackendTasks: Keep diagnostics local/static by default; do not add an ingestion backend unless a later privacy/operations decision approves it.
validationSteps: Simulate route, catalog, asset, offline, storage, and unexpected errors; review the generated report for privacy and reproduce at least one report from a clean environment.
deliverables: Operations/telemetry decision, support bundle schema, error/report UI, issue template, redaction guidance, and health-check runbook.
risksAndRollback: If telemetry cannot be operated transparently and minimally, omit it; fall back to user-submitted redacted diagnostics and scheduled checks.

researchTasks: Review search-engine requirements for client-rendered GitHub Pages, canonical/base-path behavior, sitemap/robots, valid structured data, social preview licensing, and route metadata sources.
setupSteps: Prepare representative complete/missing-media/unknown records, deployment-host configuration, metadata snapshot tests, and a crawler/link-check environment.
implementationSteps: Generate deterministic titles/descriptions/canonicals/Open Graph data, licensed fallback social images, sitemap, robots policy, valid structured data, and 404/base-path handling.
uiUxTasks: Keep document titles and shared previews descriptive and honest; avoid exposing spoilers or uncertain facts in metadata without policy.
dataBackendTasks: Generate metadata from reviewed catalog view models during build; do not introduce a rendering server solely for speculative SEO.
validationSteps: Test representative metadata snapshots, canonical URLs, sitemap links, structured-data validation, social image existence/permission, and deployed direct links.
deliverables: Metadata generator/hooks, sitemap/robots, structured-data policy, social image strategy, automated tests, and deployed validation evidence.
risksAndRollback: Remove invalid structured data or unlicensed previews immediately; retain correct basic title/description/canonical metadata as the safe baseline.

researchTasks: Confirm all release blockers, content/legal audits, operational prerequisites, community boundaries, and rollback ownership before public preview.
implementationSteps: Sequence deploy, final content audit, contribution readiness, and full QA; prohibit release by checklist theater without evidence.
uiUxTasks: Require primary flow review on the deployed preview, not only local builds.
dataBackendTasks: Freeze and identify the exact catalog/asset versions included in the release and verify restoration.
validationSteps: Run the final gate, sample every release artifact, and obtain explicit maintainer sign-off for human-required decisions.
deliverables: Week 6 release-readiness report, deployment URL, version inventory, known issues, rollback evidence, and sign-off.

researchTasks: Build an acceptance matrix from the PRD, route/data contracts, legal policy, design states, security threat model, budgets, and release checklist.
setupSteps: Use a release-candidate commit/catalog/asset version, clean browser profiles, supported viewport/browser matrix, offline mode, accessibility tools, link checker, and deployment preview.
implementationSteps: Run route/content regression, primary journeys, keyboard/contrast/zoom/responsive/offline/SEO/performance/security/legal checks, broken-link/permission audit, and known-issue triage.
uiUxTasks: Verify the deployed experience for ordinary players, including recovery paths and all meaningful empty/error/missing-content states.
dataBackendTasks: Verify exact record counts/checksums, version labels, source/asset integrity, last-good snapshot, and no protected/runtime data in the public output.
validationSteps: Require every P0 acceptance row to link reproducible evidence; classify failures as blocker, approved exception, scope cut, or follow-up with owner.
deliverables: Release-readiness matrix/report, test logs, screenshots/audits, known-issues register, exception approvals, and go/no-go maintainer decision.
risksAndRollback: Do not release with unresolved legal/asset, critical accessibility/security, corrupted catalog, or broken primary-flow defects; revert to the last validated preview.

researchTasks: Review current GitHub Pages and Actions deployment guidance, base-path/routing options, environments, artifact retention, permissions, cache headers, domain options, and rollback mechanisms.
setupSteps: Create a staging/release-candidate build, configure Pages environment and least-privilege token permissions, and identify the last-good deployment artifact/commit.
implementationSteps: Implement pinned build/deploy workflow, production base path, artifact upload, smoke checks, release checklist, version display, operations notes, and documented rollback/redeploy.
uiUxTasks: Verify direct links, refresh, assets, 404/recovery, theme, mobile navigation, and error/offline states on the deployed host.
dataBackendTasks: Deploy immutable app/catalog/asset artifacts together and record checksums/versions; no server/database is required.
validationSteps: Inspect workflow permissions, reproduce build from clean checkout, deploy preview, run smoke/link checks, and execute or dry-run rollback to a known commit/artifact.
deliverables: Pinned Pages workflow, release/rollback runbook, deployed preview, artifact/version inventory, smoke evidence, and future-hosting evaluation notes.
risksAndRollback: If history routing or headers cannot meet requirements, use the documented static fallback and compensating controls rather than adding infrastructure during release week.

researchTasks: Review current charter/contribution draft, common contributor questions, issue-form capabilities, moderation capacity, legal/source evidence needs, accessibility reporting, and AI-assisted disclosure practices.
implementationSteps: Finalize CONTRIBUTING, Code of Conduct, bug/data/feature/accessibility/asset forms, source/content review checklist, maintainer boundaries, response expectations, and AI-assistance policy.
uiUxTasks: Make forms concise, accessible, and understandable without repository expertise; provide examples and avoid requesting private or proprietary data.
dataBackendTasks: Define PR-based catalog/source/asset changes and validation evidence; do not build moderation or account infrastructure for MVP.
validationSteps: Tabletop-submit a factual correction, accessibility bug, code change, and asset proposal; verify routing, required evidence, privacy, and maintainer workload.
deliverables: Contribution/community documents, issue forms, review checklists, tested example submissions, and explicit maintainer approval.
risksAndRollback: Reduce form categories or response commitments that a solo maintainer cannot sustain; close unsafe submissions with documented policy rather than improvised decisions.

researchTasks: Freeze the candidate catalog/asset manifest and identify high-impact facts, uncertain values, source links, prose, version labels, permission evidence, alt decisions, and relationship counts to audit.
setupSteps: Generate record/asset summaries, broken-reference/link reports, permission inventory, text-diff/originality review aids, and a reproducible audit checklist tied to commit/version.
implementationSteps: Review every published asset and high-impact record, sample lower-risk content, resolve or list exceptions, confirm placeholders, and produce signed catalog/media audit results.
uiUxTasks: Inspect rendered labels, unknown/uncertain states, alt text, spoilers, broken media, and content density on representative pages.
dataBackendTasks: Verify checksums, source/asset IDs, reference integrity, generated output counts, no protected files, and exact snapshot provenance.
validationSteps: Rerun validators/link checks after corrections and independently sample the final deployment against the frozen inventory.
deliverables: Final audit report with reviewer, commit, catalog/asset versions, counts, exceptions, correction links, and explicit publication sign-off.
risksAndRollback: Remove questionable content/media or hold the prior snapshot; never downgrade an unresolved licensing or high-impact accuracy defect to cosmetic severity.

researchTasks: Use production metrics, user feedback, maintenance cost, legal input availability, and MVP limitations to prioritize expansion rather than following technical novelty.
implementationSteps: Require a separate product/legal/privacy/performance/operations decision before each runtime, analytics, replay, dataset, or hosted-service program.
uiUxTasks: Keep expansion surfaces separate and user-value-led; avoid reintroducing the old debug viewer as the default product.
dataBackendTasks: Preserve static delivery until measured triggers justify services and define migration/rollback before adoption.
validationSteps: Review entry criteria and evidence for each child issue; close or defer work lacking measurable value or sustainable ownership.
deliverables: Prioritized post-MVP decision log, metrics/feedback evidence, approved next milestone, and explicit non-selected alternatives.

researchTasks: Measure demand for chart visualization, review legal input paths, current Rust/WASM capabilities, bundle cost, browser performance, accessibility, mobile UX, and maintenance boundaries.
implementationSteps: Write a new ADR/product flow, define an isolated viewer entry and typed boundary, then integrate one legal deterministic vertical slice without coupling wiki pages to runtime internals.
uiUxTasks: Design import/input consent, unsupported-chart diagnostics, controls, loading/error states, keyboard operation, reduced motion, and a clear return to wiki content.
dataBackendTasks: Keep processing local-first by default; do not upload chart/replay data or add a backend without a separate privacy decision.
validationSteps: Benchmark bundle/startup/rendering, test supported/unsupported/legal fixtures, accessibility, failure isolation, and verify wiki routes remain unaffected.
deliverables: Approved runtime ADR/PRD, isolated entry/bundle contract, legal fixture path, prototype, benchmarks, tests, and maintenance/rollback plan.
risksAndRollback: If legal inputs, accessibility, or performance are unresolved, keep runtime local/developer-only and preserve the stable wiki release.

researchTasks: Define supported AFF constructs, malformed classes, licensing/creation provenance, minimization rules, and gaps in the current regression corpus.
implementationSteps: Create hand-authored/minimized legal fixtures with provenance metadata, expected diagnostics/scenes, naming conventions, and contribution guidance.
uiUxTasks: Include fixtures that exercise user-facing diagnostics and boundary states without requiring protected visuals or audio.
dataBackendTasks: Keep fixtures repository-local and deterministic; do not fetch official charts during tests.
validationSteps: Run parser/timing/renderer tests, mutation/minimization review, duplicate coverage analysis, and license/provenance audit.
deliverables: Expanded legal fixture corpus, provenance index, expected outputs, coverage matrix, and contributor documentation.
risksAndRollback: Remove any fixture with uncertain origin immediately and replace it with a minimal hand-authored equivalent.

researchTasks: Inventory AFF syntax from permitted documentation and current fixtures, user value, implementation complexity, error-recovery behavior, and unsupported construct frequency.
implementationSteps: Publish a support matrix and phased parser plan with exact syntax, diagnostics, tests, non-goals, compatibility, and legal fixture requirements.
uiUxTasks: Define how unsupported or partially supported charts are explained without silent misrendering.
dataBackendTasks: Keep parser domain logic in Rust and expose versioned DTOs rather than duplicating parsing in TypeScript or a service.
validationSteps: Review each proposed construct against fixtures/tests and reject roadmap items without a legal reproducible case.
deliverables: AFF support matrix, prioritized parser milestones, diagnostic policy, fixture dependencies, and acceptance-test catalog.
risksAndRollback: Prefer explicit unsupported diagnostics to permissive incorrect parsing; gate compatibility changes behind contract versions.

researchTasks: Define timing/projection/visibility invariants, official-physics uncertainty, deterministic SVG/scene expectations, performance thresholds, and current coverage gaps.
implementationSteps: Add focused timing, snapshot, scene-IR, SVG, property/regression, and benchmark tests without claiming unsupported official fidelity.
uiUxTasks: Cover visual failure states and stable debug/production distinctions needed by any future viewer UI.
dataBackendTasks: Keep renderer output deterministic and browser-independent; benchmark Rust/WASM boundary serialization separately.
validationSteps: Run full Rust/WASM/frontend regression, inspect golden changes, verify determinism across repeated runs, and document known model differences.
deliverables: Expanded test suite, invariant documentation, reviewed goldens, performance baseline, and known-limitations update.
risksAndRollback: Do not update goldens blindly; revert behavior changes lacking an explained invariant or approved model decision.

researchTasks: Identify player questions, mathematically defensible metrics, required chart inputs, explainability, bias/misuse risks, computational cost, and comparable research—not leaderboard novelty.
implementationSteps: Specify each metric formally, implement deterministic Rust calculations, expose versioned results with explanations/limitations, and add one player-facing analysis prototype.
uiUxTasks: Show definitions, contributing factors, uncertainty, and comparisons without presenting subjective difficulty claims as objective truth.
dataBackendTasks: Run locally from legal chart inputs by default; persist or aggregate nothing without a separate privacy/platform decision.
validationSteps: Use hand-calculated fixtures, invariance/property tests, sensitivity analysis, performance benchmarks, and expert review of interpretation.
deliverables: Metric specification, deterministic implementation, tests, explainability UI, limitations/bias document, and versioned contract.
risksAndRollback: Remove metrics that cannot be explained or validated; never infer player skill or difficulty from unsupported proxies.

researchTasks: Audit current JSON envelopes, duplicated TypeScript types, version negotiation, serialization cost, error taxonomy, compatibility needs, and generated-binding options.
implementationSteps: Define contract versioning/compatibility, generate or validate TS types from Rust-owned schemas, harden envelopes/errors, and isolate public runtime entry points.
uiUxTasks: Map typed boundary errors to actionable accessible UI states without exposing internal details.
dataBackendTasks: Keep browser/runtime contracts independent from wiki catalog contracts and avoid service coupling.
validationSteps: Add compatibility fixtures, schema/type drift checks, malformed response tests, serialization benchmarks, and old/new consumer tests.
deliverables: Contract ADR, generated/validated types, compatibility matrix, hardened boundary, tests, and migration guide.
risksAndRollback: Retain the previous contract adapter during migration; reject silent breaking changes.

researchTasks: Measure catalog size, update/contribution volume, review latency, dispute rate, provenance burden, storage/build time, and maintainer capacity after MVP.
implementationSteps: Design contribution queues, validation/review states, immutable snapshot publication, provenance history, governance, corrections, and rollback only for demonstrated scale needs.
uiUxTasks: Make review status, freshness, uncertainty, and corrections understandable without exposing contributor-private data.
dataBackendTasks: Evaluate repository/CI-first scaling before a database; define import/export and snapshot contracts so any future system remains replaceable.
validationSteps: Pilot with representative contribution volume, measure maintainer time and failure recovery, and test snapshot reproducibility/rollback.
deliverables: Measured scale report, approved operating model, pilot workflow, governance/provenance rules, snapshot design, and rollback evidence.
risksAndRollback: If operations exceed solo capacity, limit accepted scope/update cadence rather than automate unreviewed publication.

researchTasks: Measure static search latency, bundle/index size, update frequency, collaboration, personalization, privacy, uptime, cost, observability, and operational constraints before considering hosted services.
setupSteps: Create reproducible load/query/update scenarios and compare free/low-cost GitHub Student or open-source options against the static baseline.
implementationSteps: Write an ADR with explicit triggers, compare no-backend/static enhancements/serverless/API/database options, define API/data ownership, auth/privacy only if required, migration, deployment, observability, backup, and rollback.
uiUxTasks: Define degraded/offline behavior and ensure the public catalog remains usable when the optional service is unavailable.
dataBackendTasks: Prefer stateless search or read APIs before persistence; keep canonical exports/versioned snapshots so the service can be rebuilt or removed.
validationSteps: Benchmark a prototype against acceptance thresholds, cost ceiling, failure modes, rate limits, security/privacy review, backup restore, and static fallback.
deliverables: Measured trigger report, options matrix, ADR, prototype if justified, API/data contracts, cost/operations model, security/privacy review, and rollback plan.
risksAndRollback: Choose no backend when benefits do not exceed operational cost; preserve and test the static fallback before any cutover.

researchTasks: Identify supported legal replay/import formats, player value, sensitive fields, consent, browser storage/quota, deletion, threat model, runtime requirements, and misuse risks.
implementationSteps: Write privacy/product ADR, implement local parsing/storage lifecycle and explicit consent/deletion, then integrate one isolated analysis flow without default upload or tracking.
uiUxTasks: Explain what is read/stored, unsupported/corrupt inputs, progress, deletion, export, and privacy status; keep controls keyboard-accessible.
dataBackendTasks: Keep replay/personal data local-first and encrypted only when a demonstrated local threat requires it; hosted sync requires a separate security/privacy decision.
validationSteps: Test consent/no-consent, malformed/large inputs, quota failure, refresh/recovery, deletion verification, privacy inspection, and runtime isolation.
deliverables: Privacy/product ADR, supported-format contract, local-first prototype, lifecycle tests, threat model, user documentation, and deletion evidence.
risksAndRollback: Disable import and clear local schema/versioned storage when privacy or format safety cannot be guaranteed; never silently retain or upload data.
